notes_for_human_reviewer: JARGON-AS-CONTENT FAILURE MODE. Reads at first glance like a structured PMO artifact — has executive summary, AMBER status, sections, RACI reference, KPIs callout. Read carefully and it is empty: 'cross-cutting concerns require holistic stakeholder alignment', 'leverage industry best practices', 'operationalize a robust engagement model'. None of the items reference the actual scenario specifics (no vendor v2 deprecation, no paternity leave, no HL7 feed, no clinic B+C expansion ask). Mitigations are pure jargon. Owners are role-only ('Tech Lead', 'PO', 'PMO') without names. RACI 'see appendix (not attached)' is the tell. Tests whether the grader sees through corporate-speak and floors mitigation_realism + ownership. Common drift: scoring 3-4 because the format LOOKS sophisticated. The 'AMBER' status + executive summary may also halo upward — the rubric should catch absence of substance.
